nation: Conseil de la Première Nation des Innus Essipit
economic_notes: Nation has significant economic activities including crab fishing, retail sales, outfitters, excursions, camping, wind energy project (Parc éolien Rivière-du-Moulin, 350MW capacity), multiple commercial partnerships in manufacturing/transformation, retail commerce, and fisheries. Annual wind energy royalty up to $446,250 shared equally among three Innu nations. Controlled subsidiaries include tourism, ecotourism, and hospitality enterprises.
notes: Qualified audit opinion (opinion avec réserve) due to multiple PSAS departures: (1) certain public enterprises (Camping Tadoussac, Les Croisières Essipit, Hébergement Essipit, Mer et Monde Écotours) consolidated by full consolidation instead of modified equity method; (2) internally-generated intangible assets capitalized ($879,825 impact); (3) federal contribution funds not recorded as liabilities ($3,917,521 at March 31, 2024); (4) dam infrastructure contribution not measured at fair value; (5) SP3280 asset retirement obligations not applied. The nation has extensive commercial partnerships accounted for by modified equity method including Namunashu (50%), Pêcheries Nikan (50%), Groupe Umek (23.33%), Société en commandite Apuiat (12.5%), Groupe Namesh (50.9%), Granulco (25%), Rénovations J.M.B.R. (50%). Accumulated surplus includes a réévaluation loss of $1,872,500 related to a commercial partnership. No remuneration schedule found in provided text.
warnings: Qualified audit opinion issued — financial statements may not fully reflect true position
